Category: Technology & Telecommunications: Information Technology, Science & Technology, Software, Hardware, Telecom
Who is this for: Network administrators, IT managers, telecommunications technicians, system administrators, IT support specialists, network engineers, operations managers
Team or Dept this is for: IT, Operations, Telecommunications, Network Operations Center (NOC), Infrastructure
Description: Risks of over-automation in telecom environments.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with telecommunications concepts and a general understanding of how networks operate will help you get the most from this course, though no advanced technical expertise is required.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with over-automation in telecom environments and explain how autonomous network operations can introduce vulnerabilities when human oversight is reduced. You will also apply practical strategies to recognise warning signs of excessive automation, helping you to balance self-healing network capabilities with responsible, human-informed decision-making.
